# Pr 75983 Deferred Aggregation Telemetry Lattice And Path Sampling

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_49215834c17d4f0c98d4ad11f62f0c9a
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
deferred-aggregation-telemetry-lattice-and-path-sampling

Motivation: reduce alert noise and per-request cost attribution overhead for long-tail token spikes by introducing a 'telemetry lattice' for low-cardinality cross-layer linkage and a deferred aggregation path to compact frequent short-lived series. This PR implements a sample-first path-sampling strategy that preserves token-level latency/cost fidelity for high-value routes while preventing cardinality explosion for low-volume routes.

What changed (summary): added a lightweight trace-linking propagator (rw.telemetry.lattice) to stitch gateway request paths to runtime spans, implemented a deferred aggregator that batches metric writes at host-local level and flushes on configurable windows to the central TSDB, introduced sampled token-cost histograms by request_path, and added a new dashboard + tuned alert rules. Also includes an e2e synthetic spike test and rollout flags for staged canary.

Key design notes: telemetry lattice uses a stable 64-bit path-hash encoded in trace baggage and a short-lived sample ID for high-fidelity attribution when sample==true. Deferred aggregation reduces immediate Prometheus series churn by aggregating micro-bursts into a small set of summarized buckets before export. Path-sampling policy: always-sample for routes with route.metadata.telemetry.priority == high, probabilistic sample (1%) for medium, and adaptive sample for low (rate-limited with burst tokens).

Implementation highlights: 1) observability/metrics: token_latency_by_path (histogram_v2) and token_cost_sampled(counter with labels: path_hash,sample_id), host-local deferred_aggregator that coalesces writes, 2) tracing: propagator/propagator.go adds baggage entry "rw-tele-lattice", runtime side attaches sampled token_cost meta to the trace when present, 3) dashboards: dashboards/request-telemetry-lattice.json shows sampled vs aggregated cost and series-count impact, 4) alerts: alerts/adaptive-slo-rules.yaml replaces a static threshold for token-burn with an adaptive baseline keyed on historical p95/p99 for the route and a minimum absolute floor to avoid missing hard regressions.

Testing and rollout: unit tests added for aggregator flush logic and propagator encoding, e2e test telemetry_spike_test.go simulates synthetic bursts across 50 routes and validates series count reduction and attribution signal retention. Feature gated behind config flag telemetry.lattice.enabled and rollout guideline: 10% canary -> 50% -> all, monitor series_count and alert_firing_rate.

Backwards compatibility: non-sampled requests carry only the path_hash which is low-cardinality; no downstream schema changes required for existing dashboards.

Notes to reviewers: focus on cardinality and exporter performance, confirm that the sampling rates/flags are adequate, and validate the adaptive alert thresholds won't hide emergent regressions.

Related: ENG-4092 (alert noise reduction), ENG-4178 (token-cost attribution), minor docs update to explain operator flags.

Aisha Kapoor: Concerned about cardinality for 'path_hash' + dynamic labels; please ensure path_hash is scoped to a service and truncated consistently.
Jordan Park: Reply: path_hash is 64-bit stable hash of normalized path template and we scope exporter labels to 'service' so series = service:path_hash only. added normalization tests.
Dmitri Petrov: The deferred aggregator flush interval looks safe but we should ensure worst-case memory bounds under sustained bursts.
Jordan Park: Reply: added circuit-breaker to aggregator that forces flush and drops to summarized bucket if memory exceeds threshold; documented in telemetry-lattice.md.
Marisol Chen: Please validate that adaptive alert won't mask regressive p99 spikes where absolute latency increases but relative baseline remains similar.
Jordan Park: Reply: adaptive alert includes an absolute floor (configurable) to ensure hard regressions are still surfaced; added test covering regression scenario.
Introduce telemetry lattice for low-cardinality path linkage, sampled token-cost histograms, deferred aggregation to reduce TSDB series churn, and adaptive SLO alerting. Feature gated and rolled out by canary.
Observed prometheus series reduction ~18% in canary run and alert firing rate reduced by ~42% on noisy low-volume endpoints. Operators should monitor exporter_queue_size and aggregator_memory.
